methods.name: ClosestPoint(Point3d, Double)
methods.signature: public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double t)
methods.summary: Finds parameter of the point on a curve that is closest to testPoint. If the maximumDistance parameter is > 0, then only points whose distance to the given point is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

params.name: testPoint
params.doc: Point to search from.

params.name: t
params.doc: Parameter of local closest point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: ClosestPoint(Point3d, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool ClosestPoint(Point3d testPoint, out double t, double maximumDistance)
methods.summary: Finds the parameter of the point on a curve that is closest to testPoint. If the maximumDistance parameter is > 0, then only points whose distance to the given point is <= maximumDistance will be returned. Using a positive value of maximumDistance can substantially speed up the search.

params.name: testPoint
params.doc: Point to project.

params.name: t
params.doc: parameter of local closest point returned here.

params.name: maximumDistance
params.doc: The maximum allowed distance. Past this distance, the search is given up and false is returned.Use 0 to turn off this parameter.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: DuplicateShallow
methods.signature: public GeometryBase DuplicateShallow()
methods.summary: Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

returns.doc: An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

methods.name: Fair
methods.signature: public Curve Fair(double distanceTolerance, double angleTolerance, int clampStart, int clampEnd, int iterations)
methods.summary: Fairs a curve object. Fair works best on degree 3 (cubic) curves. Attempts to remove large curvature variations while limiting the geometry changes to be no more than the specified tolerance.

params.name: distanceTolerance
params.doc: Maximum allowed distance the faired curve is allowed to deviate from the input.

params.name: angleTolerance
params.doc: (in radians) kinks with angles <= angleTolerance are smoothed out 0.05 is a good default.

params.name: clampStart
params.doc: The number of (control vertices-1) to preserve at start. 0 = preserve start point1 = preserve start point and 1st derivative2 = preserve start point, 1st and 2nd derivative

params.name: clampEnd
params.doc: Same as clampStart.

params.name: iterations
params.doc: The number of iterations to use in adjusting the curve.

returns.doc: Returns new faired Curve on success, null on failure.

methods.name: GetNextDiscontinuity(Continuity, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, out double t)
methods.summary: Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

params.name: continuityType
params.doc: Type of continuity to search for.

params.name: t0
params.doc: Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity() and step through the discontinuities.

params.name: t1
params.doc: (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 it will be ignored unless continuityType is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.

params.name: t
params.doc: If a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

returns.doc: Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): true if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): true if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

methods.name: GetNextDiscontinuity(Continuity, Double, Double, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool GetNextDiscontinuity(Continuity continuityType, double t0, double t1, double cosAngleTolerance, double curvatureTolerance, out double t)
methods.summary: Searches for a derivative, tangent, or curvature discontinuity.

params.name: continuityType
params.doc: Type of continuity to search for.

params.name: t0
params.doc: Search begins at t0. If there is a discontinuity at t0, it will be ignored. This makes it possible to repeatedly call GetNextDiscontinuity() and step through the discontinuities.

params.name: t1
params.doc: (t0 != t1) If there is a discontinuity at t1 it will be ignored unless continuityType is a locus discontinuity type and t1 is at the start or end of the curve.

params.name: cosAngleTolerance
params.doc: default = cos(1 degree) Used only when continuity is G1_continuous or G2_continuous. If the cosine of the angle between two tangent vectors is <= cos_angle_tolerance, then a G1 discontinuity is reported.

params.name: curvatureTolerance
params.doc: (default = ON_SQRT_EPSILON) Used only when continuity is G2_continuous. If K0 and K1 are curvatures evaluated from above and below and |K0 - K1| > curvature_tolerance, then a curvature discontinuity is reported.

params.name: t
params.doc: If a discontinuity is found, then t reports the parameter at the discontinuity.

returns.doc: Parametric continuity tests c = (C0_continuous, ..., G2_continuous): true if a parametric discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1. Note well that all curves are parametrically continuous at the ends of their domains. Locus continuity tests c = (C0_locus_continuous, ...,G2_locus_continuous): true if a locus discontinuity was found strictly between t0 and t1 or at t1 is the at the end of a curve. Note well that all open curves (IsClosed()=false) are locus discontinuous at the ends of their domains. All closed curves (IsClosed()=true) are at least C0_locus_continuous at the ends of their domains.

methods.name: RibbonOffset(Double, Double, Point3d, Vector3d, Double)
methods.signature: public Curve RibbonOffset(double distance, double blendRadius, Point3d directionPoint, Vector3d normal, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Offsets a closed curve in the following way: pProject the curve to a plane with given normal. Then, loose Offset the projection by distance + blend_radius and trim off self-intersection. THen, Offset the remaining curve back in the opposite direction by blend_radius, filling gaps with blends. Finally, use the elevations of the input curve to get the correct elevations of the result.

params.name: distance
params.doc: The positive distance to offset the curve.

params.name: blendRadius
params.doc: Positive, typically the same as distance. When the offset results in a self-intersection that gets trimmed off at a kink, the kink will be blended out using this radius.

params.name: directionPoint
params.doc: A point that indicates the direction of the offset. If the offset is inward, the point's projection to the plane should be well within the curve. It will be used to decide which part of the offset to keep if there are self-intersections.

params.name: normal
params.doc: A vector that indicates the normal of the plane in which the offset will occur.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Used to determine self-intersections, not offset error.

returns.doc: The offset curve if successful.

Finally, use the elevations of the input curve to get the correct elevations of the result.

methods.name: Simplify
methods.signature: public Curve Simplify(CurveSimplifyOptions options, double distanceTolerance, double angleToleranceRadians)
methods.summary: Returns a geometrically equivalent PolyCurve. The PolyCurve has the following properties 1. All the PolyCurve segments are LineCurve, PolylineCurve, ArcCurve, or NurbsCurve. 2. The NURBS Curves segments do not have fully multiple interior knots. 3. Rational NURBS curves do not have constant weights. 4. Any segment for which IsLinear() or IsArc() is true is a Line, Polyline segment, or an Arc. 5. Adjacent co-linear or co-circular segments are combined. 6. Segments that meet with G1-continuity have there ends tuned up so that they meet with G1-continuity to within machine precision.

params.name: options
params.doc: Simplification options.

params.doc: A distance tolerance for the simplification.

params.doc: An angle tolerance for the simplification.

methods.signature: public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(int mainSegmentCount, int subSegmentCount, double maxAngleRadians, double maxChordLengthRatio, double maxAspectRatio, double tolerance, double minEdgeLength, double maxEdgeLength, bool keepStartPoint)
methods.summary: Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

params.name: mainSegmentCount
params.doc: If mainSegmentCount <= 0, then both subSegmentCount and mainSegmentCount are ignored. If mainSegmentCount > 0, then subSegmentCount must be >= 1. In this case the NURBS will be broken into mainSegmentCount equally spaced chords. If needed, each of these chords can be split into as many subSegmentCount sub-parts if the subdivision is necessary for the mesh to meet the other meshing constraints. In particular, if subSegmentCount = 0, then the curve is broken into mainSegmentCount pieces and no further testing is performed.

params.name: subSegmentCount
params.doc: An amount of subsegments.

params.name: maxAngleRadians
params.doc: ( 0 to pi ) Maximum angle (in radians) between unit tangents at adjacent vertices.

params.name: maxChordLengthRatio
params.doc: Maximum permitted value of (distance chord midpoint to curve) / (length of chord).

params.name: maxAspectRatio
params.doc: If maxAspectRatio < 1.0, the parameter is ignored. If 1 <= maxAspectRatio < sqrt(2), it is treated as if maxAspectRatio = sqrt(2). This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of (length of longest chord) / (length of shortest chord).

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: If tolerance = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of the distance from the curve to the polyline.

params.name: minEdgeLength
params.doc: The minimum permitted edge length.

params.name: maxEdgeLength
params.doc: If maxEdgeLength = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted edge length.

params.name: keepStartPoint
params.doc: If true the starting point of the curve is added to the polyline. If false the starting point of the curve is not added to the polyline.

returns.doc: PolylineCurve on success, null on error.

methods.name: ToPolyline(Int32, Int32, Double, Double, Double, Double, Double, Double, Boolean, Interval)
methods.signature: public PolylineCurve ToPolyline(int mainSegmentCount, int subSegmentCount, double maxAngleRadians, double maxChordLengthRatio, double maxAspectRatio, double tolerance, double minEdgeLength, double maxEdgeLength, bool keepStartPoint, Interval curveDomain)
methods.summary: Gets a polyline approximation of a curve.

params.name: mainSegmentCount
params.doc: If mainSegmentCount <= 0, then both subSegmentCount and mainSegmentCount are ignored. If mainSegmentCount > 0, then subSegmentCount must be >= 1. In this case the NURBS will be broken into mainSegmentCount equally spaced chords. If needed, each of these chords can be split into as many subSegmentCount sub-parts if the subdivision is necessary for the mesh to meet the other meshing constraints. In particular, if subSegmentCount = 0, then the curve is broken into mainSegmentCount pieces and no further testing is performed.

params.name: subSegmentCount
params.doc: An amount of subsegments.

params.name: maxAngleRadians
params.doc: ( 0 to pi ) Maximum angle (in radians) between unit tangents at adjacent vertices.

params.name: maxChordLengthRatio
params.doc: Maximum permitted value of (distance chord midpoint to curve) / (length of chord).

params.name: maxAspectRatio
params.doc: If maxAspectRatio < 1.0, the parameter is ignored. If 1 <= maxAspectRatio < sqrt(2), it is treated as if maxAspectRatio = sqrt(2). This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of (length of longest chord) / (length of shortest chord).

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: If tolerance = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted value of the distance from the curve to the polyline.

params.name: minEdgeLength
params.doc: The minimum permitted edge length.

params.name: maxEdgeLength
params.doc: If maxEdgeLength = 0, the parameter is ignored. This parameter controls the maximum permitted edge length.

params.name: keepStartPoint
params.doc: If true the starting point of the curve is added to the polyline. If false the starting point of the curve is not added to the polyline.

params.name: curveDomain
params.doc: This sub-domain of the NURBS curve is approximated.

returns.doc: PolylineCurve on success, null on error.

methods.remarks: See Barrett O'Neill, Elementary Differential Geometry, page 69.

properties.name: Degree
properties.summary: Gets the maximum algebraic degree of any span or a good estimate if curve spans are not algebraic.

properties.name: Dimension
properties.summary: Gets the dimension of the object. The dimension is typically three. For parameter space trimming curves the dimension is two. In rare cases the dimension can be one or greater than three.

properties.name: Disposed
properties.summary: Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.

properties.name: Domain
properties.summary: Gets or sets the domain of the curve.

properties.name: HasBrepForm
properties.summary: Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object

properties.name: HasUserData
properties.summary: Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.

properties.name: IsClosed
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is a closed curve.

properties.name: IsDeformable
properties.summary: true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.

properties.name: IsDocumentControlled
properties.summary: If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.

properties.name: IsPeriodic
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this curve is considered to be Periodic.

properties.name: IsSubDFriendly
properties.summary: Returns true if the curve is a cubic, non-rational, uniform NURBS curve that is either periodic or has natural end conditions. Othewise, false is returned.
properties.remarks: A "natural" spline has zero 2nd derivatives (and hence zero curvature) at the start and end. A "periodic" spline has unclampled periodic knots and periodic control points.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Tests an object to see if it is valid.

properties.name: ObjectType
properties.summary: Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.

properties.name: PointAtEnd
properties.summary: Evaluates point at the end of the curve.

properties.name: PointAtStart
properties.summary: Evaluates point at the start of the curve.

properties.name: PointCount
properties.summary: Gets the number of points in this polyline.

properties.name: SpanCount
properties.summary: Gets the number of non-empty smooth (c-infinity) spans in the curve.

properties.name: TangentAtEnd
properties.summary: Evaluate unit tangent vector at the end of the curve.

properties.name: TangentAtStart
properties.summary: Evaluates the unit tangent vector at the start of the curve.

properties.name: UserData
properties.summary: List of custom information that is attached to this class.

properties.name: UserDictionary
properties.summary: Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use sharable set of information.

properties.name: UserStringCount
properties.summary: Gets the amount of user strings.
